content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Accepted the amendment. It identifies a real correctness bug in checkpoint filtering for tracked ignored and assume-unchanged paths with non-UTF-8 filenames.

## Votes

- `fr-2-8798c01f`: Accept
  `git ls-files -z` produces raw pathname bytes, but the implementation in `src/adapters/worktree.rs` converts those entries with `String::from_utf8_lossy` and later feeds the rewritten UTF-8 text back into `git update-index --force-remove`. That is not a lossless round-trip on Unix for non-UTF-8 filenames, so the removal step can target a different path than the one present in the temporary checkpoint index. The consequence is real behavioral breakage: tracked gitignored or assume-unchanged files with invalid UTF-8 bytes can remain in the checkpoint tree even though the code intends to strip them. The existing tests only exercise UTF-8 names, so this regression would not be caught without the proposed coverage. Preserving raw path bytes or `OsStr`/`OsString` values through the Git round-trip is the right remediation.
